title: A General Introduction to Psychoanalysis
description: A General Introduction to Psychoanalysis — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "精神分析引论" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents for Introductory Lectures on Psychoanalysis'

memes.label: Alfred Adler
memes.description: 'Austrian physician and psychotherapist, an early associate of Sigmund Freud who later broke away to found the school of Individual Psychology, centered on the concepts of inferiority feeling and the nervous character.'

memes.label: Ambivalence
memes.description: 'The simultaneous presence of hostile and affectionate feelings toward the same person, a term coined by Eugen Bleuler and adopted by Freud.'

memes.label: Ambroise-Auguste Liébeault
memes.description: 'French physician and co-founder of the Nancy School of hypnosis, whose demonstrations with Bernheim in 1889 deeply impressed Freud.'

memes.label: "Anxiety"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, anxiety is a transformed affect arising when repressed libido is denied its normal discharge — distinguished from realistic fear by its lack of an adequate external danger and recognized as "a nodal point at which the most various and important questions converge."'

memes.label: Anxiety Neurosis
memes.description: 'A diagnostic category introduced by Freud designating a form of "actual neurosis" caused by somatic sexual factors rather than psychical conflict, distinct from the psychoneuroses.'

memes.label: "Carl Jung"
memes.description: 'Swiss psychiatrist, early leader of the Zurich school of psychoanalysis under Eugen Bleuler, who later developed analytical psychology and broke with Freud over the nature of the libido.'

memes.label: Castration
memes.description: 'A central psychoanalytic concept referring to the unconscious fear of genital injury, manifested in dream symbolism, symptom formation, and as a pivotal moment in the Oedipus complex.'

memes.label: "Dream Censor"
memes.description: 'The dream censor is the psychic agency that distorts, suppresses, or obliterates unacceptable unconscious wishes in the manifest dream, operating analogously to a political censor who deletes or rewrites offending passages in a newspaper.'

memes.label: "Clark University"
memes.description: 'The American university in Worcester, Massachusetts, where Sigmund Freud delivered his celebrated 1909 lectures on psychoanalysis — his only visit to the United States — at the invitation of G. Stanley Hall.'

memes.label: "Complex"
memes.description: 'In psychoanalysis, a complex is a structured group of emotionally charged ideas, memories, and impulses — partially or wholly unconscious — that exerts a determining influence on conscious thought, dream formation, and neurotic symptom production.'

memes.label: "Compulsion"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, compulsion neurosis is a psychoneurosis in which repressed impulses return as obsessive ideas, compulsive acts, and ritualized doubt — distinguished from hysteria by the presence of a powerful driving energy and the mechanism of undoing rather than conversion.'

memes.label: Condensation
memes.description: 'The first and primary process of the dream-work, by which multiple latent dream-thoughts are compressed into a single manifest dream element.'

memes.label: Constitutional Factor
memes.description: 'The innate, hereditary component in the etiological equation of neurosis — the constitutional predisposition that, together with accidental experiences, determines whether and in what form a neurosis develops.'

memes.label: "Day Dream"
memes.description: 'Day dreams are waking phantasies in which the individual imaginatively fulfills ungratified wishes — closely allied to night dreams in structure and motive, they provide the raw material for both neurotic symptoms and poetic creation.'

memes.label: Dementia Praecox
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, dementia praecox (later termed schizophrenia by Bleuler) is a narcissistic neurosis characterized by the withdrawal of libido from external objects back onto the ego, producing delusions of grandeur, autistic withdrawal, and a distinctive loss of reality contact.'

memes.label: Displacement
memes.description: 'A primary mechanism of the dream-work in which psychic emphasis is shifted from important to seemingly trivial elements, serving as a form of dream censorship.'

memes.label: Dream Distortion
memes.description: 'In Freudian dream theory, dream distortion refers to the process by which the latent dream thoughts are transformed into the manifest dream — the dream as recalled — rendering unacceptable unconscious wishes unrecognizable to the dreamer''s waking consciousness through the operations of the dream censorship.'

memes.label: "Dream Interpretation"
memes.description: 'The psychoanalytic method of interpreting dreams as wish-fulfillments, a central technique in Freudian theory developed in Part II of the Introductory Lectures.'

memes.label: "Dream-Work"
memes.description: 'The dream-work is the ensemble of unconscious mental operations — condensation, displacement, plastic representation, and secondary revision — that transform the latent dream-thoughts into the manifest dream content, producing the characteristic distortion and strangeness of dreams.'

memes.label: Ego
memes.description: 'The psychic agency that mediates between instinctual demands and reality; the seat of self-preservation instincts, suppression, resistance, and the conscious self.'

memes.label: Ego Instincts
memes.description: 'The instincts of self-preservation, distinguished from the sexual instincts in Freud''s dualistic drive theory.'

memes.label: Eros
memes.description: 'In Freudian metapsychology, the life instinct — the set of instincts that serve to preserve life, bind together, and create larger unities, in opposition to the death instinct (Thanatos). (020-032)'

memes.label: Eugen Bleuler
memes.description: 'Swiss psychiatrist, director of the Burghölzli clinic, coined the term "ambivalence" and contributed to the association-experiment method bridging experimental psychology and psychoanalysis.'

memes.label: "Fixation"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, fixation is the arrest of libido at an infantile stage of psychosexual development, creating a point of vulnerability to which the psyche regresses under the pressure of later privation.'

memes.label: Fore-Conscious
memes.description: 'In Freud''s topographical model of the mind, the system situated between the unconscious and consciousness, whose contents are accessible to awareness but not currently conscious.'

memes.label: Free Association
memes.description: 'The fundamental rule of psychoanalytic technique, in which the patient reports without censorship all thoughts that occur, however trivial, embarrassing, or irrelevant they may seem.'

memes.label: Hippolyte Bernheim
memes.description: 'French physician and a leading figure of the Nancy School, known for his theory of hypnotic suggestion and his influence on Freud during his 1889 visit to Nancy.'

memes.label: Homosexuality
memes.description: 'A form of sexual object-choice in which desire is directed toward persons of the same sex; classified by Freud as an inversion of the normal sexual aim rather than a degeneracy, with deep roots in universal human psychosexual development and a specific link to the narcissistic type of object-choice.'

memes.label: Hypnosis
memes.description: 'In the history of psychoanalysis, hypnosis was the precursor to Freud''s method of free association — a technique of inducing a suggestible state that temporarily overcame resistance but ultimately proved limited as a therapeutic tool.'

memes.label: Hypochondria
memes.description: 'One of Freud''s "actual neuroses," characterized by anxious preoccupation with bodily organs, explained through a narcissistic mechanism in which an organ pathologically occupies the ego''s attention.'

memes.label: "Hysteria"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, hysteria is a psychoneurosis in which repressed libidinal impulses are converted into somatic symptoms or manifest as phobic anxiety, distinguished from compulsion neurosis by the absence of amnesia and the presence of positive wish-fulfillment in symptom formation.'

memes.label: Identification
memes.description: 'A psychological process by which the ego assimilates attributes of another person, serving as a fundamental mechanism in ego development and neurotic symptom formation.'

memes.label: "Index"
memes.description: 'Index of topics from Introductory Lectures on Psychoanalysis.'

memes.label: Infantile Amnesia
memes.description: 'The normal forgetting of early childhood experiences, which Freud interprets as a continuation into later life of the infantile amnesias that conceal the beginnings of sexual life. (018-028)'

memes.label: Infantile Sexuality
memes.description: 'The sexual life of children, which begins in early infancy, is organized around component instincts, and serves as the foundation from which both normal adult sexuality and neurotic symptoms develop.'

memes.label: Jean-Martin Charcot
memes.description: 'French neurologist and professor at the Salpêtrière, Charcot was one of Freud''s teachers and a formative influence on his early career in neuropathology.'

memes.label: "Josef Breuer"
memes.description: 'Viennese physician whose treatment of Anna O. (1880–1882) and discovery of the cathartic method provided the clinical foundation for Freudian psychoanalysis.'

memes.label: Latent Dream Thoughts
memes.description: 'In Freudian dream theory, the latent dream thoughts are the unconscious ideas, wishes, and memories that constitute the true meaning of a dream — the hidden content which the dream-work transforms into the manifest dream through condensation, displacement, and other mechanisms of distortion.'

memes.label: "Libido"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, libido is the psychic energy of the sexual instincts, conceived as a quantitatively variable force that can be accumulated, displaced, transformed, and fixated — analogous to hunger in its somatic urgency.'

memes.label: Libido Theory
memes.description: 'The theoretical framework that explains psychic phenomena in terms of the distribution, transformation, and conflict of libidinal energy.'

memes.label: Megalomania
memes.description: 'A symptom characterized by grandiose self-aggrandizement, identified by Freud as a key feature of paraphrenia (dementia praecox) and linked to the withdrawal of libido from objects onto the ego.'

memes.label: Melancholia
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, melancholia is a narcissistic disorder characterized by a profound dejection, loss of interest in the external world, and self-reproach — understood as the ego''s identification with and punishment of a lost love-object.'

memes.label: narcissism
memes.description: 'The libidinal investment of the ego; a concept Freud introduced to explain the psychoses, describing the original and universal condition of libido distribution out of which object-love develops.'

memes.label: Neurasthenia
memes.description: 'One of the "actual neuroses" in Freudian nosology, alongside anxiety neurosis and hypochondria, characterized by nervous exhaustion believed to result from somatic rather than psychical causes. (024-025)'

memes.label: "Neurosis"
memes.description: 'The central clinical concept of psychoanalysis: a psychic disorder resulting from the conflict between unconscious wishes and the forces of repression, manifesting in symptoms that represent compromise formations.'

memes.label: Oedipus Complex
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, the Oedipus complex designates the child''s unconscious erotic attachment to the parent of the opposite sex and rivalry with the parent of the same sex — the nuclear complex of every neurosis and the central organizing principle of psychosexual development.'

memes.label: Otto Rank
memes.description: 'Austrian psychoanalyst, writer, and early collaborator of Sigmund Freud; known for his work on dream interpretation, the birth of the hero myth, and the application of psychoanalysis to literature and mythology.'

memes.label: Paranoia
memes.description: 'A chronic systematic insanity characterized by fixed delusional systems, which Freud analyzed as a defense against repressed homosexual wishes through the mechanism of projection.'

memes.label: Paraphrenia
memes.description: 'Freud''s classification term for the group of narcissistic disorders encompassing dementia praecox (schizophrenia) and paranoia, distinguished from the transference neuroses by the withdrawal of libido from objects onto the ego.'

memes.label: "Parapraxes"
memes.description: 'Parapraxes (Fehlleistungen) are the apparently trivial errors of everyday life — slips of the tongue, misreadings, mislayings, and forgettings — which psychoanalysis reveals to be meaningful acts determined by unconscious motives rather than accidents of inattention.'

memes.label: "Perversion"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, perversion designates a sexual activity that diverges from the genital aim of reproduction, understood not as degeneracy but as a persistence or return of infantile component instincts that are a universal part of human sexual development.'

memes.label: Phobia
memes.description: 'A form of neurotic fear bound to specific objects or situations, classified by Freud as anxiety hysteria, produced by the transformation of suppressed libido into fear and the projection of that fear onto an external danger.'

memes.label: Phylogenetic Inheritance
memes.description: 'The inherited psychic material — archaic memory-traces, primal phantasies, and phylogenetic schemata — that each individual brings into the world and that shapes the universal patterns of neurotic symptom formation and dream symbolism.'

memes.label: Pleasure Principle
memes.description: 'The governing principle of the unconscious psychic apparatus, which seeks immediate pleasurable discharge of excitation, opposed by the reality principle.'

memes.label: "Preface"
memes.description: "Preface by G. Stanley Hall to the authorized English translation of Freud's Introductory Lectures on Psychoanalysis."

memes.label: Projection
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, projection is a defense mechanism by which an internal impulse, wish, or feeling that the ego finds unacceptable is attributed to an external object or person — the subject says not "I hate him" but "he hates me" — playing a central role in paranoia, phobia formation, and the construction of external reality.'

memes.label: Psychical Excitation
memes.description: 'In Freudian metapsychology, the quantity of psychic energy or tension that flows through the mental apparatus — the "economic" dimension of mental life that seeks discharge through pleasure and accumulates as unpleasure when blocked.'

memes.label: "Psychoanalysis"
memes.description: 'Psychoanalysis is the method of investigating unconscious mental processes, the therapeutic technique based on that investigation, and the body of psychological knowledge derived from it — all founded by Sigmund Freud and introduced systematically in the Introductory Lectures.'

memes.label: "Psychoneurosis"
memes.description: 'In Freudian nosology, psychoneurosis designates neurotic conditions of psychogenic origin — as distinct from actual neuroses caused by somatic factors — characterized by unconscious conflict, symbolic symptom formation, and accessibility to psychoanalytic treatment.'

memes.label: "Regression"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, regression is the return of the libido to earlier fixation points when its forward path is blocked by privation, constituting the second major danger in the development of the libido alongside inhibition.'

memes.label: Repression
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, repression is the fundamental defense mechanism by which unacceptable ideas, wishes, or impulses are expelled from conscious awareness into the unconscious, where they continue to exert pathogenic influence — the cornerstone concept upon which the entire edifice of psychoanalysis rests.'

memes.label: "Resistance"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, resistance is the psychic force that opposes the emergence of repressed unconscious material into consciousness, manifesting most visibly in the patient during psychoanalytic treatment.'

memes.label: "Secondary Elaboration"
memes.description: 'The fourth mechanism of the dream-work: the process by which the dream acquires a surface coherence and narrative intelligibility through the ego''s rationalizing activity, modifying the raw dream material to approximate waking logical thought.'

memes.label: Sense of Guilt
memes.description: 'The unconscious feeling of guilt — derived from the Oedipus complex and intensified by repressed hostile and sexual wishes — that underlies neurotic suffering, self-punishment, and the need for punishment that resists therapeutic cure.'

memes.label: Sexual Object
memes.description: 'In Freudian theory, the person or thing toward which the sexual instinct is directed, distinguished from the sexual aim (the act the instinct strives for). The choice and fixation of the sexual object is central to the libido theory. (010-057)'

memes.label: Sexuality
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, sexuality denotes a far broader domain than the narrow colloquial sense of genital reproduction — it encompasses all the component drives of infancy, the perversions, and the libidinal energies that fuel both neurosis and culture.'

memes.label: "Sigmund Freud"
memes.description: 'Sigmund Freud (1856–1939), the founder of psychoanalysis, delivered the Introductory Lectures on Psychoanalysis at the University of Vienna between 1915 and 1917.'

memes.label: "Sublimation"
memes.description: 'Sublimation is the process by which sexual instinctual energy (libido) is diverted from its original sexual aim toward socially valued, non-sexual pursuits.'

memes.label: Substitute Formation
memes.description: 'A distorted psychic representation that stands in for an inaccessible unconscious content — the fundamental mechanism by which the dream, the symptom, and the parapraxis disguise their true meaning.'

memes.label: Suggestion
memes.description: 'The therapeutic method of directly commanding the removal of symptoms through the authority of the physician, contrasted by Freud with psychoanalytic therapy.'

memes.label: "Suppression"
memes.description: 'Suppression (Unterdrückung) is the conscious or preconscious act of holding down an unacceptable impulse — distinct from the unconscious mechanism of repression (Verdrängung), though the two are closely allied in the genesis of neurotic symptoms.'

memes.label: Symbolism
memes.description: 'A mode of dream representation in which dream elements act as constant translations of latent dream thoughts, distinct from the individual dream-censor.'

memes.label: Symptom Formation
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, symptom formation designates the process by which unconscious, repressed impulses achieve a disguised, compromise expression in the form of neurotic symptoms — the central pathogenic mechanism through which psychic conflict is converted into observable clinical phenomena.'

memes.label: "The Nancy School"
memes.description: 'The school of hypnotism founded in Nancy, France, by Ambroise-Auguste Liébault and Hippolyte Bernheim, whose demonstrations of suggestion influenced Sigmund Freud early in his career.'

memes.label: "The Study of Hysteria"
memes.description: 'The clinical investigation of hysteria by Josef Breuer and Sigmund Freud (1880–1895) that led to the discovery of the cathartic method, the concept of unconscious meaning in symptoms, and the founding of psychoanalytic therapy.'

memes.label: "The Unconscious"
memes.description: 'The cornerstone of Freudian psychoanalysis: the hypothesis that psychic processes are predominantly unconscious and that consciousness is merely a partial aspect of mental life.'

memes.label: "The Zurich School of Psychoanalysis"
memes.description: 'The group of psychiatrists at the Burghölzli Hospital in Zurich, led by Eugen Bleuler and Carl Jung, who adopted and extended psychoanalysis to the study of the psychoses in the early 1900s, providing crucial academic legitimacy to the psychoanalytic movement.'

memes.label: "Transference"
memes.description: 'In psychoanalytic therapy, transference is the process by which the patient redirects libidinal impulses originally directed toward infantile objects onto the person of the analyst, transforming the treatment relationship into a live reenactment of the patient''s pathogenic past.'

memes.label: "Transference Neurosis"
memes.description: 'In Freudian psychoanalysis, the transference neurosis is the artificial neurosis that develops during treatment as the patient redirects libidinal investments from the original neurotic symptoms onto the person of the analyst, making the pathogenic conflict accessible to therapeutic intervention in the here-and-now of the analytic situation.'

memes.label: Trauma
memes.description: 'An experience that overwhelms the ego''s capacity for mastery, producing a fixation of libido at the moment of the traumatic event and serving as a key etiological factor in the neuroses.'

memes.label: Wilhelm Wundt
memes.description: 'German philosopher and founder of experimental psychology. His association-experiment method was adapted by the Zurich School into a bridge to psychoanalysis.'

memes.label: Wish Fulfillment
memes.description: 'The thesis that every dream represents the fulfillment of an unconscious wish, forming the essential character of the dream and the key to its interpretation.'
